accurate.why: All dollar amounts, percentages, and program descriptions match the bill text accurately.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or incorrect claims; the contextual description of counter-UAS authority is accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the act extends expiring FAA authorizations and aviation taxes through May 10-11, 2024.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes that the act amends title 49, the Internal Revenue Code, and the Homeland Security Act of 2002.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the obligation authority extending through September 30, 2024, and the 39% apportionment reduction condition.

effective_dates.why: Correctly identifies the May 10 and May 11, 2024, extension dates and the October 1, 2023, period start.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers AIP funding, apportionment methodology, FAA operations, facilities, R&D, EAS, trust fund expenditure authority, aviation taxes, and counter-UAS extension.

coherent.why: Well-organized by title with logical structure.

concise.why: Very detailed; lists numerous specific programs and dollar figures that exceed what a summary requires.

correct_entities.why: FAA, DOT, Homeland Security Act of 2002, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, Essential Air Service correctly named.

correct_figures.why: AIP amount ($2.04B), FAA operations ($7.26B), facilities ($1.79B), R&D ($155M), EAS ($216M), 39% reduction, and dates all match the bill text.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
